title: Arka tracks on S2 Works
wikitext:
[]: Arka tracks on S2 Works             Quote Post by Atropos » Mon May 12, 2014 8:08 pm I read somewhere in this forum that Anno had Sagisu compose tracks for "Arka, the promised land," and that this music is in S2 Works. I have that album, but I don't know which tracks they are, and Google is failing me. Since I heard it here, figured I might come back. Anyone willing to help?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 12, 2014 8:59 pm Here's our wiki page's track listing: https://wiki.evageeks.org/S2_Works, https://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:S%5E2_Works_Table_of_Contents I think this is what you're looking for, so I hope that helps.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon May 19, 2014 8:02 pm Wow. So these tracks were meant for something different. Well Anno used them wisely later.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 19, 2014 9:08 pm Well, the original ideas for Eva would have made it a different show ...

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue May 20, 2014 5:58 pm I read that a lot of time ago. It really is pretty different, but I wonder if Anno already planned on centering the show so much on the minds of the characters. There is mention of "psychic attacks" by angels. And even though Anno once said he just wanted to make a robot show, in the "What we are trying to do" essay he spends like half the time talking about the characters and how emotionally damaged they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Jun 06, 2014 6:46 pm I'm a bit late in responding, but thank you. Tolkien once observed that tales tend to grow in the telling. I think that's what happened with Eva, but instead of going outwards, Anno chose to go inward, focusing on introspection—to the show's benefit, in all honesty.

[]: Arka tracks on S2 Works             Quote Post by Atropos » Mon May 12, 2014 8:08 pm I read somewhere in this forum that Anno had Sagisu compose tracks for "Arka, the promised land," and that this music is in S2 Works. I have that album, but I don't know which tracks they are, and Google is failing me. Since I heard it here, figured I might come back. Anyone willing to help?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 12, 2014 8:59 pm Here's our wiki page's track listing: https://wiki.evageeks.org/S2_Works, https://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:S%5E2_Works_Table_of_Contents I think this is what you're looking for, so I hope that helps.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon May 19, 2014 8:02 pm Wow. So these tracks were meant for something different. Well Anno used them wisely later.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 19, 2014 9:08 pm Well, the original ideas for Eva would have made it a different show ...

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue May 20, 2014 5:58 pm I read that a lot of time ago. It really is pretty different, but I wonder if Anno already planned on centering the show so much on the minds of the characters. There is mention of "psychic attacks" by angels. And even though Anno once said he just wanted to make a robot show, in the "What we are trying to do" essay he spends like half the time talking about the characters and how emotionally damaged they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Jun 06, 2014 6:46 pm I'm a bit late in responding, but thank you. Tolkien once observed that tales tend to grow in the telling. I think that's what happened with Eva, but instead of going outwards, Anno chose to go inward, focusing on introspection—to the show's benefit, in all honesty.
